title: Merge Conflict Resolution
description: Two developers edited the same function in `app.js` on different branches. A merge conflict is waiting for you. Resolve it correctly: keep both developers' changes by combining them, then complete the merge.
objective: Branch `main` must contain a merge commit that incorporates changes from both `feat/logging` and the original `main`. The file `app.js` must contain both the logging line and the error handling line. The working tree must be clean.
hints: Open the conflicted file and look for `<<<<<<<`, `=======`, and `>>>>>>>` markers.
You need to keep lines from both sides — edit the file manually to combine them, then remove the markers.
After resolving, `git add <file>` to mark it resolved, then `git merge --continue` or `git commit`.
